message:
Initial release of narcolepsyd

Idle power optimizer for Linux laptops with Intel hybrid CPUs.
Auto-detects CPU topology and parks cores when user is idle.
Display stays on, instant restore on input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,103 @@
+name: Release
+
+on:
+  push:
+    tags:
+      - 'v*'
+
+permissions:
+  contents: write
+
+jobs:
+  build-deb:
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    strategy:
+      matrix:
+        include:
+          - target: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu
+            arch: amd64
+
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+        with:
+          targets: ${{ matrix.target }}
+
+      - name: Build
+        run: cargo build --release --target ${{ matrix.target }}
+
+      - name: Get version
+        id: version
+        run: echo "version=${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
+
+      - name: Build .deb
+        run: |
+          VERSION=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
+          PKG_DIR="narcolepsyd_${VERSION}_${{ matrix.arch }}"
+
+          mkdir -p "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN"
+          mkdir -p "${PKG_DIR}/usr/bin"
+          mkdir -p "${PKG_DIR}/usr/lib/systemd/system"
+
+          cp target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/narcolepsyd "${PKG_DIR}/usr/bin/"
+          chmod 755 "${PKG_DIR}/usr/bin/narcolepsyd"
+          cp dist/narcolepsyd.service "${PKG_DIR}/usr/lib/systemd/system/"
+
+          cat > "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/control" << EOF
+          Package: narcolepsyd
+          Version: ${VERSION}
+          Section: admin
+          Priority: optional
+          Architecture: ${{ matrix.arch }}
+          Maintainer: Jesse Vincent <jesse@fsck.com>
+          Description: Idle power optimizer for Linux laptops with Intel hybrid CPUs
+           narcolepsyd monitors user input and aggressively parks CPU cores
+           when idle to reduce power consumption. Display stays on. All
+           changes are reverted instantly on any keyboard or touchpad input.
+           Supports Intel Alder Lake, Raptor Lake, Meteor Lake, Lunar Lake,
+           and newer hybrid CPUs.
+          Homepage: https://github.com/obra/narcolepsyd
+          EOF
+
+          # Fix indentation (heredoc adds leading spaces)
+          sed -i 's/^          //' "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/control"
+
+          cat > "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/postinst" << 'POSTINST'
+          #!/bin/sh
+          set -e
+          if [ "$1" = "configure" ]; then
+              systemctl daemon-reload
+              systemctl enable narcolepsyd.service || true
+              systemctl start narcolepsyd.service || true
+          fi
+          POSTINST
+          sed -i 's/^          //' "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/postinst"
+          chmod 755 "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/postinst"
+
+          cat > "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/prerm" << 'PRERM'
+          #!/bin/sh
+          set -e
+          if [ "$1" = "remove" ] || [ "$1" = "deconfigure" ]; then
+              systemctl stop narcolepsyd.service || true
+              systemctl disable narcolepsyd.service || true
+          fi
+          PRERM
+          sed -i 's/^          //' "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/prerm"
+          chmod 755 "${PKG_DIR}/DEBIAN/prerm"
+
+          dpkg-deb --build --root-owner-group "${PKG_DIR}"
+
+      - name: Upload .deb to release
+        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
+        with:
+          files: narcolepsyd_*.deb
+
+      - name: Upload binary to release
+        run: |
+          VERSION=${{ steps.version.outputs.version }}
+          cp target/${{ matrix.target }}/release/narcolepsyd "narcolepsyd-${VERSION}-linux-${{ matrix.arch }}"
+
+      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
+        with:
+          files: narcolepsyd-*-linux-*

+[package]
+name = "narcolepsyd"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+description = "Idle power optimizer for Linux laptops with Intel hybrid CPUs"
+license = "Apache-2.0"
+repository = "https://github.com/obra/narcolepsyd"
+readme = "README.md"
+
+[dependencies]
+libc = "0.2"
+log = "0.4"
+env_logger = "0.11"
+
+[profile.release]
+opt-level = "s"
+lto = true
+strip = true

@@ -0,0 +1,120 @@
+# narcolepsyd
+
+An idle power optimizer for Linux laptops with Intel hybrid CPUs.
+
+narcolepsyd monitors keyboard and touchpad input. When you stop interacting with your laptop, it aggressively parks CPU cores and reduces power consumption. When you touch any input device, everything is restored instantly.
+
+Your display stays on. Your network stays connected. There's no visible flicker or delay.
+
+## Why
+
+Modern Intel hybrid CPUs (Alder Lake and newer) have three tiers of CPU cores: Performance (P-cores), Efficiency (E-cores), and Low-Power Efficiency (LP E-cores). Linux keeps all of them powered and ready even when you're staring at a static screen.
+
+Windows has Intel DPTF/DTT, a proprietary framework that coordinates power management across CPU, GPU, and display. Linux doesn't have an equivalent. narcolepsyd bridges part of that gap.
+
+On a Fujitsu LIFEBOOK UH with a 31 Wh battery, narcolepsyd reduced idle system power by measurably extending battery life during typical use (reading, thinking, waiting for builds).
+
+## What it does
+
+When idle (no keyboard/touchpad input for 3 seconds):
+
+- **Parks P-cores** and most E-cores, keeping only 2 E-cores + LP E-cores online
+- **Caps remaining core frequencies** to 800 MHz
+- **Sets EPP** (Energy Performance Preference) to maximum power saving on all cores
+- **Disables turbo boost**
+- **Suspends non-essential USB devices** (webcam, fingerprint reader)
+
+When you press a key or touch the trackpad:
+
+- All cores come back online (typically <50ms)
+- Frequencies, EPP, and turbo are restored to their previous values
+- USB devices are resumed
+
+## Supported hardware
+
+Any Linux laptop with an Intel hybrid CPU using the `intel_pstate` driver:
+
+- **Alder Lake** (12th Gen) — Core i5/i7/i9-12xxx
+- **Raptor Lake** (13th/14th Gen) — Core i5/i7/i9-13xxx/14xxx
+- **Meteor Lake** (Core Ultra Series 1) — Core Ultra 5/7/9 1xxU/H
+- **Lunar Lake** (Core Ultra Series 2) — Core Ultra 5/7/9 2xxV
+- **Arrow Lake** (Core Ultra Series 2) — Core Ultra 5/7/9 2xxH/HX
+- **Panther Lake** and newer — should work automatically
+
+narcolepsyd auto-detects your CPU topology. No configuration needed.
+
+## Install
+
+### From .deb (Ubuntu/Debian)
+
+Download the latest `.deb` from [Releases](https://github.com/obra/narcolepsyd/releases):
+
+```bash
+sudo dpkg -i narcolepsyd_*.deb
+sudo systemctl enable --now narcolepsyd
+```
+
+### From source
+
+```bash
+cargo build --release
+sudo cp target/release/narcolepsyd /usr/local/bin/
+sudo cp dist/narcolepsyd.service /etc/systemd/system/
+sudo systemctl daemon-reload
+sudo systemctl enable --now narcolepsyd
+```
+
+## Usage
+
+narcolepsyd runs as a systemd service. It requires root (for CPU hotplug and `/dev/input` access).
+
+```bash
+# Check status
+systemctl status narcolepsyd
+
+# View logs
+journalctl -u narcolepsyd
+
+# Verbose logging (debug transitions)
+sudo RUST_LOG=debug narcolepsyd
+```
+
+### Signals
+
+- `SIGTERM` / `SIGINT` — graceful shutdown, restores all power settings
+- Power state is always restored on exit, even on crash
+
+## Configuration
+
+narcolepsyd currently has no configuration file. It uses sensible defaults:
+
+| Setting | Value |
+|---------|-------|
+| Idle timeout | 3 seconds |
+| E-cores kept online | 2 |
+| Low-power frequency cap | 800 MHz |
+| Only on battery | Yes |
+
+These will be configurable in a future release.
+
+## How it works
+
+1. Opens `/dev/input/event*` devices and identifies keyboards and pointing devices
+2. Uses `poll(2)` to wait for input events with zero CPU overhead while waiting
+3. After 3 seconds of silence, writes to sysfs to offline cores, cap frequencies, and set EPP
+4. On any input event, immediately restores everything via sysfs
+5. Periodically checks AC power state and inhibitor processes (zoom, ffmpeg, etc.)
+
+The daemon itself consumes effectively zero CPU — it spends all its time blocked in `poll(2)`.
+
+## Safety
+
+- **Always restores state on exit** — SIGTERM, SIGINT, and panics all trigger cleanup
+- **Won't run on AC power** — only activates on battery
+- **Respects inhibitors** — won't park cores while zoom, ffmpeg, vlc, or similar are running
+- **CPU 0 is never offlined** — Linux requires at least one CPU online
+- **Display is never touched** — no screen blanking, no suspend, no flicker
+
+## License
+
+Apache 2.0. See [LICENSE](LICENSE).

@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+[Unit]
+Description=narcolepsyd — idle power optimizer for Intel hybrid CPUs
+Documentation=https://github.com/obra/narcolepsyd
+After=multi-user.target graphical.target
+
+[Service]
+Type=simple
+ExecStart=/usr/bin/narcolepsyd
+Restart=on-failure
+RestartSec=10
+Nice=19
+IOSchedulingClass=idle
+
+[Install]
+WantedBy=multi-user.target
